title: Save Motor Configuration From a Working Motor in the Same Position
summary: Use Teknic ClearPath software to connect to a working motor in the same position as the replacement motor, save that motor configuration as an .mtr file, and retain the file for later upload to the replacement motor. The source states that motors used in the same position, such as Z1 or Z2, have the same parameters.
when_to_use: Use this procedure during motor replacement work when the configuration file for the motor being replaced is not already available and a working motor in the same position is available to copy from.
not_for: Do not use when a same-position working motor is not available.
Do not use to copy parameters from a motor in a different position when same-position equivalence is not supported by the source.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Identify a working same-position motor, connect the laptop and USB cable, run Teknic ClearPath, save the configuration as an .mtr file, and disconnect from the motor.

safety_notes: This procedure is associated with motor replacement activity and is not framed by the source as a normal operator task.
Use only a working motor in the same position because the source states motors in the same position have the same parameters.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the motor USB connection
A working motor in the same position or the motor being replaced

access_required: Access to the motor USB connection
Access to a laptop with Teknic ClearPath application
system_preconditions: A working motor in the same position is available if the configuration file for the motor being replaced is not already available.

visual_references.description: Page 122 maintenance diagram associated with motor replacement workflow and the motor USB port cover / USB connection point used for configuration transfer.
Figure 7-5 identifying the Teknic SD Motor referenced in the motor replacement and parameter save workflow.

steps.title: Confirm a working same-position motor is available
steps.purpose: Ensure the source-supported parameter equivalence applies before saving a configuration for reuse.
steps.instruction: Identify whether the replacement motor position is the same as another working motor position, such as Z1 or Z2. Use a working motor in the same position because the source states motors used in the same position have the same parameters.
steps.expected_result: A suitable working motor in the same position is identified as the source for the configuration file.
steps.healthy_condition: A working motor in the same position is available and selected.
steps.failure_condition: No working same-position motor is available, or position equivalence cannot be established from the source-supported example.

actions.label: Identify same-position motor
actions.exact_action: Identify whether the replacement motor position is the same as another working motor position, such as Z1 or Z2, because motors used in the same position have the same parameters.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Identify the Teknic SD motor referenced by the motor replacement and parameter save procedure.

stop_or_escalate_if: A same-position working motor is not available.
It is not possible to determine that the available motor is in the same position as the replacement motor.

steps.title: Connect the USB cable between the motor and laptop
steps.purpose: Establish the physical connection needed for Teknic ClearPath to access the motor configuration.
steps.instruction: If the configuration file for the motor being replaced is not already available, connect a USB cable to the motor and the laptop.
steps.expected_result: The motor and laptop are connected by USB and ready for software access.
steps.healthy_condition: The USB cable is connected at the motor connection point and at the laptop.
steps.failure_condition: The USB connection cannot be made or the motor USB access point cannot be located.

actions.label: Connect USB cable
actions.exact_action: Connect a USB cable to the motor and the laptop.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Locate the motor USB port cover and the motor connection point for the USB cable.

stop_or_escalate_if: The configuration file is not already available and the USB connection cannot be established.
The motor USB port cover or connection point cannot be located from the available source-supported visual reference.

steps.title: Run Teknic ClearPath
steps.purpose: Open the manufacturer application used to access and save the motor configuration.
steps.expected_result: Teknic ClearPath is open and available for saving the motor configuration.
steps.healthy_condition: The Teknic ClearPath application launches successfully.
steps.failure_condition: The Teknic ClearPath application cannot be run.
stop_or_escalate_if: The Teknic ClearPath application is not available or cannot be launched.

steps.title: Save the motor configuration as an .mtr file
steps.purpose: Capture the working motor parameters in a reusable file for the replacement motor.
steps.instruction: From the File menu, select Save Configuration to save the configuration as an .mtr file.
steps.expected_result: The motor configuration is saved as an .mtr file.
steps.healthy_condition: An .mtr configuration file is created and available for later upload to the replacement motor in the same position.
steps.failure_condition: The configuration cannot be saved or no .mtr file is produced.

actions.label: Save configuration file
actions.exact_action: From the File menu, select Save Configuration to save the configuration as an .mtr file.

stop_or_escalate_if: The configuration cannot be obtained from the existing motor.

steps.title: Disconnect from the motor
steps.purpose: End the connection after the configuration file has been saved.
steps.instruction: Disconnect from the motor after the configuration file is saved.
steps.expected_result: The motor is disconnected and the saved .mtr file is retained for reuse.
steps.healthy_condition: The motor is disconnected only after the .mtr file has been saved.
steps.failure_condition: The motor remains connected unnecessarily or the file was not saved before disconnecting.

actions.label: Disconnect motor connection
actions.exact_action: Disconnect from the motor after the configuration file is saved.

stop_or_escalate_if: The configuration file was not successfully saved before disconnecting.

success_criteria: A motor configuration file is saved as an .mtr file.
The saved .mtr file is available for upload to a replacement motor in the same position.
healthy_conditions: A working motor in the same position is available.
The USB cable can be connected between the motor and laptop.
The configuration is saved as an .mtr file.
failure_conditions: A same-position working motor is not available.
The configuration cannot be obtained from the existing motor.
The USB connection cannot be established.
The Teknic ClearPath application cannot be run.
The configuration cannot be saved as an .mtr file.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if a same-position working motor is not available.
Escalate if the configuration cannot be obtained from the existing motor.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Motor USB port cover location and motor connection point for the USB cable.
Teknic SD motor referenced by the motor replacement and configuration save procedure.

missing_details: The source packet does not provide a time estimate for this specific procedure.
The source packet does not explicitly state whether production stop is required.
The source packet does not explicitly state whether LOTO is required for this specific configuration save sequence.
The source packet does not provide exact file naming or storage location guidance for the .mtr file.
The source packet does not provide detailed software screen captures for the Teknic ClearPath File menu.
